author: Alexey Milovidov
message:
Give the fuzzer server the same 120s readiness budget

Address review: the AST/BuzzHouse fuzzer bootstrap in `run-fuzzer.sh`
gave the server only ~30 seconds to answer `select 1` before the next
`clickhouse-client` under `set -e` aborted the job, while the fuzzer
jobs run the same `amd_debug` and sanitizer artifacts that motivated
raising the `wait_ready` deadline in `clickhouse_proc.py`. Raise the
loop to 120 attempts and break early when the server process died, as
`wait_for_server` in `perf/compare.sh` does, so crashed servers are
still detected quickly.

PR: https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/pull/110287

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Groene AI
message:
Fix 02907_suggestions_readonly_user flakiness: wait for prompt before next command

The .expect script matched the query result ("Ok.") and immediately sent the
next command without waiting for the interactive prompt to be redrawn. Under
sanitizer + parallel CI load the send could race the readline redraw and get
lost, so a later "expect eof" never completed and the test timed out.

Add "expect ":) "" after each command's result, matching the idiom already
used by 02417_repeat_input_commands and 01945_show_debug_warning. Revert the
earlier timeout bump (60s is sufficient once the sequencing is correct).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
